economic_notes: First Nation receives 93% of revenue from Government of Canada and First Nation and Inuit Health. Nibinamik Power Authority and Nibinamik Education Authority consolidated. Wasaya Partnership accounted for by modified equity method. Portfolio investment in Exchange Income Corporation held at cost. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event with potential impact on capital projects and funding.
notes: Audit is qualified on two grounds: (1) inability to observe physical inventories ($850,901) due to access limitations; (2) insufficient audit evidence for carrying value of investment in First Nation business partnerships ($179,486) and Exchange Income Corporation portfolio investment ($182,721). Consolidated entities include Nibinamik Power Authority and Nibinamik Education Authority. Wasaya Partnership accounted for by modified equity method (1 GBE). Financial statements were completed very late - audit letter dated May 25, 2022 and auditor's report dated May 10, 2022 for fiscal year ended March 31, 2020 (approximately 2 years late). OSR calculated as total revenue $23,683,790 minus total government transfers $22,125,693 = $1,558,097. Annual debt service represents net advances of long-term debt per cash flow statement; principal repayments not separately disclosed. Forgivable loans of $4,750 outstanding. First Nation guaranteed up to $130,000 to suppliers of Nibinamik Community Store and also acts as guarantor for Happy Time Tours & Travel borrowings.
